import_sp_h2: #1 Import the world into Minecraft (Single Player)
import_sp_para1: When importing the world for single player, only you - or the user on your local device - will be able to play. To play with other players, see section #2 below.
import_sp_para2: To load the world in single player, follow these steps after downloading:
import_sp_step1: Right-click the downloaded .zip file "Projekt City [V5.0]" and select "Extract All".
import_sp_step2: Click "Extract" in the bottom right of the dialog window.
import_sp_step3: Wait for the extraction to complete, then return to your previous directory.
import_sp_step4: Right-click the extracted folder and copy it.
import_sp_step5: Press the Windows key, type "run", and open the Run application. Enter "%appdata%" and click "OK".
import_sp_step6: Navigate to ".minecraft" → "saves" and paste the copied folder there. Make sure there is no additional folder nested inside - if the world folder contains another folder within it, the world may not load correctly.
import_mp_h2: #2 Import the world onto a Minecraft server (Multiplayer)
import_mp_step1: Right-click the downloaded .zip file "Projekt City [V5.0]" and select "Extract All".
import_mp_step2: Click "Extract" in the bottom right of the dialog window.
import_mp_step3: Wait for the extraction to complete, then return to your previous directory.
import_mp_step4: Right-click the extracted folder and copy it.
import_mp_step6: Paste the copied folder into the root directory of your server.
import_mp_step7: Rename the folder to "world" if necessary. If an existing "world" folder is present, remove it first. Please note: deleting your existing world folder permanently removes all progress made on that server world. This action cannot be undone.
import_mp_para: You can also add the world as an additional world using the Multiverse Core plugin. Apex Hosting provides a detailed step-by-step guide for this at the following address:
